title: Mentzelia integra
intro: Mentzelia integra, commonly known as the whole-leaf blazing star or whole-leaf stickleaf, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Loasaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Mentzelia integra has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Mentzelia integra is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
taxonomy: Mentzelia integra is classified within the family Loasaceae. This family, also known as the lovage family, consists of about 130 species of flowering plants. The genus Mentzelia is a group of flowering plants native to North America. Mentzelia integra is further classified as a perennial or annual plant within the genus, with specific characteristics that distinguish it from other species. The exact classification within the Mentzelia genus may require further research.
